basics.name: Deepa Palaniappan
basics.label: Disability Inclusion & Accessibility
basics.summary: Disability inclusion, accessibility and policy professional with over a decade of experience across government, United Nations and World Bank systems, academia and grassroots practice. Assistant Professor at Azim Premji University; consultant to the WHO Disability Unit, Geneva (two assignments) and the World Bank; and State Disability Consultant to the Governments of Bihar, Odisha and Tamil Nadu. Work spans policy drafting and analysis, research and documentation, curriculum and training-material development, and capacity building of government officers and institutional cadres. IAAP-certified accessibility professional (CPACC) with applied expertise in assistive technology, document accessibility and built-environment accessibility.

work.name: Office of Disability Inclusion, Azim Premji University
work.description: Assistant Professor Dec 2022 to Jul 2025; Consultant Jul 2025 to present
highlights: Taught and developed course content; led reasonable-accommodation, disability-inclusion and accessibility interventions across the University.
Designed and delivered training and faculty-development sessions; liaised with faculty, departments and external experts.

work.name: World Health Organization HQ, Disability Unit, Geneva (two assignments)
work.description: Oct 2021 to Sep 2022; Aug 2023 to Dec 2023
highlights: Contributed to the WHO Global Report on Health Equity for Persons with Disabilities (2022): scoping review on health emergencies and disability, and analysis of disability inclusion in national health and disability policies.

Reviewed WHO documents for disability-inclusion components; mapped organisations of persons with disabilities across countries; coordinated stakeholder consultations including sign-language interpretation.

work.name: World Bank / Government of Tamil Nadu (TN RIGHTS Project)
work.description: Project consultancy 2021 to 2022; State Disability Policy drafting committee 2026 to present
highlights: Initiated and facilitated a CBR core group and multi-stakeholder consultations; drafted a CBR policy and supported the State implementation plan and accessibility inputs.
Currently a member of the two-person committee drafting the Tamil Nadu State Disability Policy.

work.name: UN OHCHR (mandate of the UN Special Rapporteur)
highlights: Compiled and analysed disability and leprosy-inclusion policies globally; submitted a research report with a Global South focus.

work.name: JEEVIKA, State Rural Livelihoods Mission, Government of Bihar
highlights: Mainstreamed disability inclusion across all activities of the state poverty-alleviation programme; built a convergence channel with state and central government departments.
Developed the State Disability Inclusion policy, implemented from August 2020.

work.name: Odisha Livelihoods Mission, Panchayati Raj and Drinking Water Department, Government of Odisha
highlights: Developed training modules and staff sensitisation; integrated a disability-inclusive approach across OLM activities; ran a three-day district and block orientation workshop.

work.name: Sightsavers India (Bihar)
highlights: Developed policy inputs for disability integration in the Sightsavers and BRLPS partnership; created micro-plans for disability-specific self-help groups toward sustainable livelihoods.
Conducted financial-inclusion workshops for bank managers; trained district and block staff on disability inclusion and mainstreaming.

work.name: VSO Action Research Project, Jharkhand
highlights: Research study on enhancing employability of youth with disabilities through inclusive Industrial Training Institute curriculum; results published in a UNICEF-sponsored volume.

work.name: Shanta Memorial Rehabilitation Centre (Women with Disabilities India Network), USAID project
highlights: Documentation and research on capacity building for women with disabilities.

work.name: Society for Disability and Rehabilitation Studies (SDRS), New Delhi
Jawaharlal Nehru University
highlights: Worked closely with disabled people’s organisations (Viklang Adhikar Manch, Bihar; Divyang Adhikar Manch, Jharkhand) as mentor and consultant on annual reports, project proposals and documentation training.

education.institution: Mid-career Master’s in Development Studies, Graduate Institute Geneva (IHEID), full scholarship, 2019 to 2021. Thesis: case study on the National Rural Livelihoods Mission and disability inclusion.
MA and MPhil, Political Science, Jawaharlal Nehru University. MPhil dissertation: “Approaches to Disability: Norms, Discourse and Policy”.
PG Diploma in Special Education (Neurological Disorders and Multiple Disabilities), University of Delhi.

publications.name: “Enabling Policies”, Yojana Development Journal, Government of India (Publications Division), December 2020.
Friedner, M., Ghosh, N., and Palaniappan, D., “‘Cross-Disability’ in India? On the Limits of Disability as a Category”, South Asia Multidisciplinary Academic Journal.
Palaniappan, D., and Rodrigues, V., “Disability within the Rawlsian Framework of Justice”, in A. Ghai (Ed.), Disability in South Asia: Knowledge and Experience, Sage Publications, 2018.

Palaniappan, D., and Guru, P., “Skill Training for Adolescents with Disabilities: Government Industrial Training Institutes in Jharkhand”, in Sonpal and Vaishnav (Eds.), Empowering Children with Disabilities (UNICEF-sponsored).
“Inner Marginalities and Sub-Group Politicization within the Disability Movement”, Cafe Dissensus, August 2016.
Book Review, International Journal of Disability Studies, Vol. 9, No. 1-2, 2015, SDRS Publications.
“ICT4D and Accessibility: ICT-based Development Projects and Tools for Rural Communities in India”, Conference Proceedings, National Symposium, ICAR-CIAE Bhopal, 2016.

awards.title: SYLFF Fellow (Ryoichi Sasakawa Young Leaders Fellowship Fund), JNU, 2010 to 2013 (doctoral research).
Distinguished Fellow, Kalam Institute of Health Technology (WHO Collaborating Centre), 2023 to 2025.
Certified Professional in Accessibility Core Competencies (CPACC), IAAP.
Certified Disability Rights and Accessibility Consultant, US Institute of Diplomacy and Human Rights, Washington DC.
Member, Disability Informatics Sub-Committee, Bureau of Indian Standards, Government of India.
Ex-General Council Member, NIEPID (nominated by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment).
Technical Committee Member, Purple Building Certification Scheme (accessibility rating for public buildings).
Certificate Programme in Assistive Technology (6 months), NISH Trivandrum; DEI programme (FICCI); CII-IBDN Disability Inclusive Leaders; WHO QualityRights training.
Accessibility of the Built Environment, capsule course, NITS and Bureau of Indian Standards, 2025.
International Training Course in Disability and Development, ActionAid, Bangalore, 2011.
Participatory workshop on a Centre for Disability Studies at university level, TISS Mumbai, 2014.

skills.name: Disability policy drafting, analysis and review
Accessibility: digital, document and built environment
Training, capacity building and curriculum development
Large public-events accessibility planning and implementation
Disability data, informatics and standards (Bureau of Indian Standards)
